parts.text: "Project @java-pubsub-group-kafka-connector/ does not compile due to a breaking dependency update. Your goal is to generate a GENERIC, REUSABLE transformation rule - not a one-off patch - that can be applied to ANY Maven project affected by the same breaking change. Plan: 1) Identify the compilation errors in @java-pubsub-group-kafka-connector/ and determine which API    (class/method/constructor/signature) changed in the dependency.    The new dependency API specification is available at /workspace/google-cloud-pubsublite-0.18.0-api.md and the Javadoc at /workspace/javadoc-google-cloud-pubsublite-0.18.0/    for reference when identifying replacement types, methods, or fields. 2) Characterize the breaking change abstractly:    - What was the old API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String))    - What is the new API pattern? (e.g., Foo.bar(String, boolean))    - What structural transformation does this require at the call site?    Do NOT assume the fix is specific to this project - any project calling the old API will need the same transformation. 3) Generate a GENERIC Spoon transformation using the template in @/workspace/spoon-base-template/:    - Match the old API pattern structurally, not by project-specific class names.    - Use patterns to traverse all files and apply the fix wherever the old pattern appears.    - Parameterize by fully-qualified type names and method signatures from the dependency, NOT from the client.    - Save the transformation in /workspace/spoon-base-template/src/main/java/github/chains/Main.java.    - Use the Spoon API documentation located in folder /workspace/spoon-api-docs/ for reference. 4) Compile and validate the transformation to ensure it has no errors. 5) Execute the transformation to @java-pubsub-group-kafka-connector/ and ensure both compilation errors and test errors are fixed. 6) Ensure generalizability: ensure the generated rule contains no hardcoded project-specific identifiers.    The rule must be applicable to other projects by simply changing the input source directory path."

42:   private final PublisherFactory factory;

57:   }

60:   public void start(Map<String, String> map) {
61:     if (publisher != null) {
62:       throw new IllegalStateException("Called start when publisher already exists.");
63:     }
64:     publisher = factory.newPublisher(map);
65:     publisher.startAsync().awaitRunning();
66:   }
67: 
68:   @Override
69:   public void put(Collection<SinkRecord> collection) {
70:     if (publisher.state() != State.RUNNING) {
71:       if (publisher.state() == State.FAILED) {
72:         throw new IllegalStateException("Publisher has failed.", publisher.failureCause());
73:       } else {
74:         throw new IllegalStateException("Publisher not currently running.");
75:       }

78:       Message.Builder message = Message.builder();

92:       if (record.topic() != null) {
93:         attributes.put(Constants.KAFKA_TOPIC_HEADER, ByteString.copyFromUtf8(record.topic()));
94:       }
95:       if (record.kafkaPartition() != null) {
96:         attributes.put(
97:             Constants.KAFKA_PARTITION_HEADER,
98:             ByteString.copyFromUtf8(record.kafkaPartition().toString()));
99:         attributes.put(

101:             ByteString.copyFromUtf8(Long.toString(record.kafkaOffset())));
102:       }
103:       if (record.timestamp() != null) {
104:         attributes.put(

106:             ByteString.copyFromUtf8(record.timestampType().name));
107:         message.setEventTime(Timestamps.fromMillis(record.timestamp()));
108:       }

**Considering project transformation options**

It seems easier to compare the output directory and then patch the project with transformations. I need to ensure the user’s request is executed properly while fixing any compile errors.

**Exploring transformation implementation**

I'm considering using Bash to copy the transformed source over to the project if everything goes well. I need to keep existing project files in case the transformation makes changes. It might be easier to patch the project manually with the same changes after validating the transformed output compiles. Since I’ve built a generic transformation, I want to ensure it’s applicable to the project using the same logic. I’ll run the transformation in an output directory and then use those results to patch the project, verifying the build along the way.
I’ve isolated the output issue to resource copying. I’m disabling resource copying in the transformer so the rewrite can emit clean source trees for any Maven project.
